title: Drip vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and Buttondown email marketing tools. Find out which is best for e-commerce, newsletters, and your budget.
intro: Drip and Buttondown serve different email marketing needs. Drip is built for e-commerce businesses seeking advanced tracking and store integration, while Buttondown caters to developers and writers who want simplicity and markdown support. Your choice depends on your business model and technical preferences.

comparison_table.tool_a: E-commerce businesses with store integration needs
comparison_table.tool_b: Developers and technical writers using markdown

comparison_table.tool_a: Revenue attribution, e-commerce tracking, SMS, automation builder
comparison_table.tool_b: Markdown editing, API access, paid subscriptions feature, higher ratings

choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce store and need revenue tracking and attribution
You want SMS marketing capabilities integrated with email
You need visual automation builder and advanced segmentation tools
choose_b_if: You prefer writing emails in markdown format
You need to start free and scale affordably as you grow
You're a developer who values API access and technical flexibility

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Drip is superior for e-commerce with its revenue attribution and store integration, while Buttondown excels for writers and developers with better ratings, lower cost, and markdown support. Drip is more feature-rich for commerce; Buttondown is simpler and more affordable.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown is significantly cheaper. It offers a free tier for up to 100 subscribers, then starts at $9/month. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. If you're just starting out, Buttondown is the clear winner.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality, making migration possible. Buttondown specifically lists import/export as a feature, so you can export your subscribers and content. However, you'll lose some functionality like markdown editing if switching to Drip's more visual interface.
